_comment: A realistic mid-stage deck: strong, well-sourced, but with real gaps. It scores in the C band, not 0 and not 100. This is the example to study -- the sloppy and sharp decks show the extremes; a real founder deck lives here, and the findings that remain (source the NRR figure, add a number to the problem slide, tighten one headline) are the ones worth a founder's time.

slides.headline: Helm ships the pull request, not the suggestion.
lines: An autonomous engineer that opens, tests, and merges changes.
slides.notes: Lead with the outcome: merged code, not autocomplete.

slides.headline: Senior engineers spend half their week in review.
lines: Review is the bottleneck once a team passes ten engineers.
Autocomplete sped up typing, not the slow part: review and rework.
slides.notes: The pain is review throughput, not keystrokes.

slides.headline: Agents crossed the merge-without-edits bar.
lines: In 2024, 40% of pull requests from Helm merged with no human edits.
$0.04 per merged change makes autonomy cheaper than the review it removes.
slides.notes: Anchor why-now in a measured capability jump, with the unit economics.

slides.headline: 10x fewer review cycles per change.
lines: Helm drives 10x fewer review cycles by testing before it asks for review.
Runs on Python 3.11 and Node 20 stacks, SOC 2 Type II from day one.
slides.notes: The version and compliance line is incidental, not a metric claim.

slides.headline: It reads the repo, writes the test, opens the PR.
lines: Every change ships with a test and a reverted-on-red guarantee.
p95 review-to-merge under five minutes on partner repos.
slides.notes: Show a real merged PR in the live demo.

slides.headline: $500B is spent on software labor each year.
lines: $500B spent on software labor each year is the real budget we attack.
We meet 100K developers where they already work: the pull request.
slides.notes: Frame the market as labor spend, not IDE seats.

slides.headline: Priced per merged change, so spend tracks output.
lines: $900K ARR with net revenue retention of 155% across teams.
Usage expands as teams route more of the backlog through Helm.
slides.notes: Retention answers the leaky-bucket question up front.

slides.headline: Why not Copilot, or the model labs?
lines: Copilot optimizes keystrokes inside the editor; we optimize merged output.
We own the test-and-merge workflow and the eval set, not the model itself.
slides.notes: Platform risk: the workflow of record and the customer evals are the moat.

slides.headline: We built CI at Vercel and GitHub.
lines: Engineers who shipped merge automation to millions of repositories.
slides.notes: Founder-market fit on the exact workflow.

slides.headline: Raising $20M to reach ~$7M ARR.
lines: Raising $20M to expand from 30 teams to the broad mid-market.
Projected ~$7M ARR within two years on current expansion rates.
slides.notes: Use of funds: eval platform and design-partner GTM.
